sketches.mechanism: The `object` keyword creates a singleton — exactly one instance can ever exist, and it is created lazily on first access.
sketches.resonance: Some people exist in your life as a singleton: you cannot create another, cannot copy them, and they only come into being the moment you reach for them.

sketches.mechanism: A `PhantomData` field makes the compiler believe a type holds data it never actually stores — a zero-size type that exists only to satisfy the type system's reasoning about lifetimes and ownership.
sketches.resonance: Grief is carrying something that takes up no space but changes every constraint on how you move through the world.

sketches.mechanism: The `never` type represents a value that can never exist; a function returning `never` is one from which no return is possible.
sketches.resonance: There are promises whose fulfillment is structurally impossible — the type system knows before you do that nothing will come back.
